title: Debt repayment
description: Practice understanding of snowball and high rate methods of debt repayment.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the snowball method of debt repayment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: paying off debts from the smallest balance to the largest balance
paying off debts with the highest interest rate first
only making minimum payments on all debts

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which debt repayment method would you use if you wanted to pay off your debts in order of balance from smallest to largest?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: only making minimum payments on all debts

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the following statement.**

The snowball method involves paying off debts from the [[☃ dropdown 1]] balance to the [[☃ dropdown 2]] balance.
hints:
Snowball method got its name because it resembles what happens to a snowball when it starts rolling downhill.

A snowball starts small and gets bigger as it continues to roll.
A snowball method follows the same logic: you start off paying small balances and move on to larger balances as time goes by.
The snowball method involves paying off debts from the **smallest** balance to the **largest** balance.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the high rate method of debt repayment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: paying off debts from the smallest balance to the largest balance
paying off debts with the highest interest rate first
only making minimum payments on all debts

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which debt repayment method would you use if you wanted to pay off your debts in order of highest interest rate first, followed by lower interest rate ones?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: making only minimum payments on all debts

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the following statement.**

The high rate method focuses on paying off debts with the [[☃ dropdown 1]]  [[☃ dropdown 2]] first.
hints: High rate method got its name based on its main focus: high interest rates.
A high rate method has you prioritize paying credit card balance with high interest rates.
High rate method focuses on paying off debts with the **highest** **interest rate** first.

assessment_items.question_content:
Paying off debt is an important step towards financial freedom. There are two popular methods for repaying debt: the snowball method and the high rate method.

**Which method is best for staying motivated during debt repayment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Paying off debt is an important step towards financial freedom. There are two popular methods for repaying debt: the snowball method and the high rate method.

**Which method saves you more money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Paying off debt is an important step towards financial freedom. There are two popular methods for repaying debt: the snowball method and the high rate method.

**Which method takes longer to see progress?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Below is a table of the loans you have currently taken out with their balance and interest rate.

Type of loan | Balance | Interest rate 
-|-|-
Mortgage | $\$150{,}000$ | $3.5\%$ 
Business loan | $\$75{,}000$ | $8\%$ 
Personal loan | $\$5{,}000$ | $12\%$ 

**If you are following the *snowball* method, which loan should you pay off *first*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Snowball method focuses on looking at loan balances to help decide which loan to pay off first.
With this method, you focus on paying off the smallest balance first.
Personal loan has the smallest balance.
**Personal loan** has the *smallest* balance and should be paid off first.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a table of the loans you have currently taken out with their balance and interest rate.

Type of loan | Balance | Interest rate 
-|-|-
Mortgage | $\$100{,}000$ | $3\%$ 
Student loan | $\$8{,}000$ | $6\%$ 
Auto loan | $\$15{,}000$ | $7\%$ 

**If you are following the *snowball* method, which loan should you pay off *first*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Snowball method focuses on looking at loan balances to help decide which loan to pay off first.
With this method, you focus on paying off the smallest balance first.
Student loan has the smallest balance.
**Student loan** has the *smallest* balance and should be paid off first.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is a table of the loans you have currently taken out with their balance and interest rate.

Type of loan | Balance | Interest rate 
-|-|-
Mortgage | $\$100{,}000$ | $5\%$ 
Personal loan | $\$7{,}000$ | $7.5\%$ 
Auto loan | $\$12{,}000$ | $11\%$ 

**If you are following the *high rate* method, which loan should you pay off *first*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: High rate method focuses on looking at loan interest rates to help decide which loan to pay off first.
With this method, you focus on paying off the loan with the highest interest rate first.
Auto loan has the highest interest rate.
**Auto loan** has the *highest* interest rate and should be paid off first.
